Guidelines on the use of Internal Models

rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:
In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

sections.chapter_title: Permission to Use Full and Partial Internal Models

rules.summary: A firm may calculate its SCR using an internal model that is either a full internal model or a partial internal model only:
A firm that has been granted _internal model permission  must calculate its SCR using the internal model_ for which internal model permission has been granted.

sections.chapter_title: Applications for Permission to Use Full and Partial Internal Models

rules.summary: A firm making an internal model permission application must either:
A firm making an internal model permission application must demonstrate to the PRA that its systems for identifying, measuring, monitoring, managing and reporting risk are adequate.
When making an internal model permission application, a firm must submit its internal model change policy to the PRA.
A firm with an internal model permission must be able to, upon request by the PRA, provide the PRA with an estimate of the SCR determined in accordance with the standard formula.

sections.chapter_title: Applications for Permission to Use Partial Internal Models

sections.chapter_title: Responsibilities of the Firm’s Governing Body

rules.summary: A firm’s :
A firm must have in place systems which ensure that its internal model operates properly on a continuous basis.
A firm with an internal model permission must not, in respect of the internal model for which that internal model permission has been granted, revert to calculating the whole or any part of the _S
If a firm with internal model permission ceases to comply with the internal model requirements , the firm must, without delay, either present to the PRA a plan to restore compliance within a r
A firm must demonstrate to the PRA that its internal model is widely used, and plays an important role in its system of governance (referred to in [Conditions Governing Business 2](/pra-rules/co

An internal model shall not be considered to be widely used in or to play an important role in the system of governance of a firm where the quantifications of risks and the risk ranking produced by th
A firm must also demonstrate to the PRA that the frequency of calculation of its SCR using the internal model is consistent with the frequency with which it uses its internal model for the p
A firm must ensure the ongoing appropriateness of the design and operations of its internal model , and that the internal model continues to appropriately reflect the risk profile of the firm.
A firm must ensure that the design of the internal model is aligned with its activities, including by ensuring that:
A firm must ensure that the internal model change policy provides that the internal model is to be adjusted for changes in the scope or nature of the activities of the firm.

Where the internal model is used for different purposes, a firm must ensure consistency between the different outputs of the internal model.

A firm must ensure that the persons who effectively run the firm have a sufficiently detailed understanding of the parts of the internal model used in the area for which they are responsible.
